content:
Welcome to the primer for the CEDH Sisay, Weatherlight Captain! 
Updated 11/12/2025

We have a discord, come join us!
https://discord.gg/23edgkp5np

===accordion

===panel: About the Deck
===accordion
===panel: Deck Builder's Note

This Build is based on years of experience and meta dependent slots based on the game as it changes. I have tested vigorously to keep this list being the leanest and most efficient version of  "Traditional" or Oath of Nicol style of Sisay. It uses planeswalker combos with tutor chains through Sisay to provide a combo that is very hard to interact with once you get the engine going. The philosophy of this deck is to become an inevitable bringer of doom to any table. What do i mean by this? Given that this deck can't help but make mana as long as you make your land drops and get legends out the win will always be nearer and nearer. Putting value on the table is the baseline of the gameplan and spin as soon as possible.

For another good repository of information, please check out The Book of Combos - linked [HERE](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Fb0jFu289YS6GlIvpf_pdBFZu-YPrbnv2bsyb00w3oI/edit?usp=drivesdk).  Credits to @Excidium for creating and maintaining that resource
===endpanel
===panel: Introduction
Hey all Tolaria East at your service! 
Some info about me, I have been playing Sisay Weatherlight Captain since it's MH1 release as well as her first printing of Captain Sisay! She was my first CEDH commander as the Paradox Engine Queen! 
At first the Sisay Weatherlight Captain started as a Planeswalker casual deck where I just got to do cool kick flips, until a good friend of mine Malcolm (Or Veiss online) showed me the combo he developed Oath of Bolas, and in my eyes made Sisay a viable deck in the CEDH meta. 
Within the past few years I have excelled with her and done my best to keep the good name of Sisay going. However i still have a lot to improve upon and work towards in keeping that goal in mind. Post ban Sisay took quite the hit but I am here to do her justice and continue to prove that she is the best 5C commander! 
If you have any questions feel free to DM me on Discord- Tolariaeast 
===endpanel
===panel: Why Sisay, and a bit of Sisay Philosophy
[[Sisay, Weatherlight Captain]]

Sisay is a five color adaptive combo deck, a tool box if you will. Meaning that no matter where we are within the game we have a gameplan and a way to over come and win. Having two main win cons that i will cover later but also having more subtle and hidden lines that one may not see on the surface. Layered within the 99 and only necessary in pivotal moments. 

Sisay has a unique ability to put legendary *permanents* onto the battlefield for [[symbol:w]][[symbol:u]][[symbol:b]][[symbol:r]][[symbol:g]] based on Sisay's power. Thanks to this ability we can tutor anything we may need in order to get the ball rolling and work towards the win without too much issue. Because of her unique ability a few things are to be kept in mind. Counterspells *typically* do not affect us, Opposition agent and cursed totem are our #1 enemies, and protection is but one spin away. 

Sisay used to be a rogue deck and nobody knew what she did. However nowadays she's consistently been ranked in the top 10 decks in all of cEDH from MtG Tournaments. After the Dockside ban some think that her best days are behind her but i do not think that is true. Dockside made her gameplan simple and effective but was the card was parasitic for our format on the whole so we had to adapt! This current build is focused on mana production and putting win attempts on the stack as efficiently as possible with that mindset of mana. 


===endpanel
===panel: Is this the deck for you?

You may like playing Sisay if:
- You like consistent performance in your decks. You have a tutor in the command zone, it’s really hard to be more consistent than that. All we need is Mana, our commander, and the ability to tutor and activate creature abilities. It’s a really low floor for what is necessary to win. 
- You want to play a “meta” deck while still playing a deck with Brewer’s advantage. There are many, many ways to build a Sisay list.  We got Pod (Shoutout to BirthingPodByll), we got Breach (Clam Shout out to Turtlepod), we got superfriends, we have so many options on how to build Sisay, you can fit whatever you want to play into your deck.
- You want to play a deck that you can swap out pieces to fill “silver bullet” needs without sacrificing slots needed to advance your own game plan. (We can play [[Oko, Thief of Crowns]], [[Ashiok, Dream Render]], or [[Ertai Resurrected]]. Even some cards like [[Jirina, dauntless general]] see play in Sisay lists.) Legendary things are extremely good for us because they make our own plan easier to accomplish while also tripping up our opponents, so we are likely the best deck to run any number of otherwise relegated legendary "pet cards" 

You will not enjoy this deck if:
- You enjoy pure turbo
- you don't like counting to WUBRG
- If you are afraid OF turbo 
- You do not like Rube Goldberg styled decks where you have to amalgamate a gameplan from one dork and a dream 
- You do not like having all the options under the sun being five color and have access to ANYTHING you want to try 
- You want to play the top tier meta dustbin copy paste nonsense and miss out on all the brain groove training you get from playing a just off meta deck like this! 

===endpanel

===endaccordion
===endpanel

===panel: Combos and Winning the Game
===accordion

===panel: How to actually get to the combos
The easiest way to attain the usual combos by Sisay is anything that can produce one of each colored mana. That could be Bloom tender, Faeburrow elder, Jegantha and even Chromatic Orrery. Once you have one of these active, beyond someone having inveraction like removal or opposition agent, you can go up the chain with ease and win unopposed through the Oath of Nicol line. However there are other ways to achieve the win which discuss soon. Through Sisay herself being the best tutor for our gameplan we also have the usual tutors. Worldly, Vampiric, Enlightened and of course Demonic tutor and depending on your preferences Eladamris call, Green Sun Zenith etc. The point is the more tutors we can resolve the more inevitable the win line becomes.

Sisay can grab quite a few of the lines by herself with a tutor on the end step before your turn, and then another tutor on your own turn (Example: End step tutor for Selvala, on your turn you tutor Derevi, untap the selvala, make more mana and tutor out emiel, activate Emiel targeting derevi, untap selvala and make infinite mana) Is one of the simplist but most effective ways to win the game. 
===endpanel

===panel: An oath sworn and the noble goal
****1. **Oath of Bolas******

This is arguably the easiest win con one can strive for while piloting this deck. Oath of Bolas refers to having [[Oath of Teferi]], [[Nicol bolas Dragon god]] and [[Aminatou the Fateshifter]] all out simultaneously. Tutor chaining into these three cards allows you to have infinite planeswalker activations, infinite mana and infinite "flickers." 
To actually close the game out with this line you can either-
1. Tutor [[Saheeli Rai]] for infinite +1 activations to ping the table out. 
2. Tutor [[Mount Doom]], flicker the land while infinite mana is floating and ping the table out.
3. through either [[Kinnan, bonder prodigy]] flips or a regular tutor, grabbing [[Orcish Bowmaster]] and flickering him to ping the table out.

How to get here- 
The easiest way to go up through the "tutor chain" process is through a rainbow dork. Whats a rainbow dork you may ask? Any creature, Planeswalker or Rock that makes at least WUBRG to activate Sisay and go up the chaining process. For example-
You have a [[Bloom tender]] that can make WUBRG and Sisays power is at least 4. Tap the BT (bloom tender) for WUBRG, tutor out [[Derevi Empyiral Tactician]], [[Tyvar Jubilant Brawler]], or [[Saheeli Rai]] All of these cards have one thing in common, they can either untap the BT or reset it with haste to go tutor again. From here, any of those choices being used, you get Aminatou to -1 activate and flicker any of them. That allows Derevi to untap BT or Reset Tyvar and Saheeli to reactivate as new game pieces and reset the BT with haste and go tutor chain again. From here you can tutor for either Oath of Teferi or Nicol Bolas. For the sake of example lets say we grab Nicol first. Upon the next activation of Sisay you tutor for Bolas, He will now activate as Aminatou because of his static ability to copy planeswalker activations. So activating as Aminatou you can -1 reset Aminatou and have Aminatou -1 again to reset any of the untappers mentioned before. Lastly you tutor out Oath of Teferi, this is the final piece to allow you to start chaining activations off Aminatou and Bolas to infinitely reset each other and have one extra activation from each of them to reset another game piece and win in the aforementioned methods above.

Mind you there are a few more ways to get to the "tutor chain" that can replace Bloom tender-
* [[Faeburrow Elder]]
* [[Chromatic Orrery]]
* [[Dihada Binder of Wills]] (depending on your set up you can do the walker activations or grab [[Cultist of the Absolute]] and then Orrery for the same effect)
* [[Mana vault]] + [[Kinnan]] + [[Agathas Soul Cauldron ]] and three excess mana
* 15 Mana and a big enough Sisay (Three activations.)



===endpanel
===panel: Flash Unicorn (Flash Wins)
**2. Flash Unicorn**

This line requires [[Emiel the Blessed]]. There are a few ways through the almighty unicorn that you can make infinite mana and even on someone else's turn win the game! 
To close the game out this is what you will require-
Emiel + [[Derevi Empyrial Tactician]] + 
1. A mana dork with haste that can produce at least 4 mana 
2. [[Gaeas Cradle]] able to produce at least 4 mana
3. Chromatic Orrery

These few cards allow you to have infinite mana at any time by Derevi untapping all of these through Emiels activation. From there to finish you can grab either-
1. [[Mount doom]] With the infinite mana and untaps from before this allows you to ping the table out using MT Dooms first ability 
2. [[Orcish Bowmasters]] - Activating Emiel flickering OBM and burning the table out. Through tutoring out Kinnan and activating him infinitely until you flip into OBM, this is the easiest way to get him at instant speed. A second way would be to infinitely flicker, get mana, get Orrery, reset orrery indefinitely and activate its second ability to draw X cards until you draw into OBM. 

There is also a "non deterministic" lock down that Emiel provides. It may not win the game necessarily but it is good if key pieces like OBM and MT Doom are gone forever and that lads is the "Ertai Lock"

The Ertai lock pertains to my GOAT [[Ertai Resurrected]]  While you have infinite flickers/mana you can grab Ertai and essentially turn the game off. His abilities read- Counter any spell, activated or triggered ability and Destroy a creature or planeswalker. With Emiels lines allowing me to have infinite mana and flickers I can make sure no one is able to do anything ever again. No spells, no activations, no channels. Nothing. Locking down the game until my turn where I can try and win through struggled measures. No this does not guarantee you the game but its a damn good way to make sure no one else is winning either. A bit of a last ditch effort kind of move here. *Not currently equipped for this combo for the time*


===endpanel
===endaccordion

===endpanel


===panel: FAQ / Single Card Discussions

===accordion
===panel: Included Non-legends
In this section lets discuss some of the Non legend slots and their uses to further your gameplan. 
* If it makes a mana, it is probably good enough to fit into this deck. That ranges from birds of paradise to Fellwar stone. Mana is the most important thing for this deck so if it can make mana you're on a good line of doing well. This includes [[Birds of paradise]], [[Deathrite Shaman]], [[Delighted halfling]], [[Tinder wall]], [[Ignoble and Noble hierarch]], [[Ruby Daring tracker]], [[Enduring vitality]], [[Bloom Tender]] [[Elvish Spirit Guide]], [[Simian Spirit Guide]], [[Faeburrow elder]], [[Chrome mox]] [[Lotus Petal]], [[mox amber]] [[Mox diamond]], [[Mox opal]], [[Mana vault]], [[Sol ring]], [[Arcane signet]], [[Fellwar stone]], [[Relic of Legends]], and [[Chromatic Orrery]] within this list. 

* [[Orcish Bowmaster]] While this card has been pivotal in stopping our green based deck, it is a necessary evil to provide a good counter card to other OBMs as well as great synergy within the deck. Beyond the infinite damage loop it provides while you do Emiel or Bolas lines it also is just a painfully good card for us. Too often I have had the army token be larger than Sisay while i have Selvala out and it allows me to go up the chain with lots of mana to spare. It also has cauldron synergy! Did your Agathas Soul Cauldron eat a Birds of Paradise? Well now that army produces mana too. Lastly OBM is a two cmc for two body count. That is supremely helpful with Enduring Vitality and Gaea's Cradle to keep numbers for mana up! 

* [[Imposter mech]] a clone that usually cant be killed! copying opponents kinnans, tymnas, hell even etalis is just a base use for this card which I have gotten miles out of, beyond that getting an unkillable OBM is nothing to overlook! 

* [[Smothering Tithe]] This card is a no brainer for any CEDH Sisay list. We are in a midrange meta full of parasitic draws and abuse of cards like Rhystic Study and The One Ring. Taking advantage of those effects is only beneficial for us. Wheel of fortunes get kept in hand, and Tymnas debate on drawing during that combat. Its a perfect mix of Stax piece and ramp for us. 

* [[faerie mastermind]] a solid draw engine that also doubles as a hidden win con along side kinnan, tithe and orcish bowmaster

* [[Mystic remora]] [[Rhystic study]] [[The One Ring]] and [[esper sentinel]] are cedh draw engines that are mainstays in any deck that can run them. Drawing can be a downside within Sisay because if we draw any of the pieces we typically tutor for it can be tricky to cast them later on but it is still important to have some measure of draw power in any competitive deck. 
===endpanel
===panel: Included Legendaries 

* [[Teferi time raveler]] one of the most important non combo legends we play. A silence through one spin. With this card out opponents can only cast spells at sorcery speed, and once your opponents let you tutor and it resolves to grab him they become *mostly* locked out of the game (Channel effects still work) plus it also doubles as a removal piece with his -3 bouncing nearly anything you could want. 

* [[Dihada Binder of Wills]] this is an important addition to the deck post dockside ban. I always made the joke saying "When/If dockside gets banned this card is an auto inclusion for the deck" Her -3 mills cards to give us nearly enough mana to win the game through the oath of nicol line, her activation and three excess mana allows us to get there. I used to be against this card given the percent chance when you activate her you could mill your important combo pieces, even though you could put it in hand, it still jumbles the plan. However now that is a necessary gamble given where the post dock ban as put us. 

* [[Ragavan Nimble Pilferer]] the best monkey in all the land. A simple card with a simple premise, deal damage get treasure. That alone allows him to be such an important legendary for our deck. Stealing cards from other peoples libraries is also fantastic for being disruptive in someone elses win won AND there is always a chance you flip a good rock or dork off someone to help further the gameplan. 

* [[Kinnan Bonder Prodigy]] Post ban we have become a "Hidden Kinnan" list. This means that typically our first low end spin for two cmc can very viably be Kinnan. With all the Mana Dorks and Rocks we Play Kinnan very quickly becomes quite the issue to deal with. Plus if all else fails when we get infinite mana spinning through Kinnans ability is another way for us to go off without needing to use Sisay. 

* [[Lotho Corrupt Shirriff]] Simple on this one, two pips towards legendary power for Sisay and makes treasures. what more could you ask for? 

* [[Wan Shi Tong Librarian]] i tried playing [[archivist of oghma]] at one point so why not test out his younger cooler bluer brother. This card has way too many words that are good for specifically Sisay. First its a blue pip legend, solid. Than it has flash, flying and vigi great for end step buffs or derevi beats for untaps. Then it gets +1/+1 counters, excellent for cauldron synergy or perhaps a bigger creature for selvala to tap from. 

* [[The cabbage merchant]] so far a strong contender against alot of the usual rainbow dorks. in games you're able to get him out soon enough you will dominate with mana pending a wild removal spell for him. 

* [[Deadpool trading card]] this new guy is all the rage, from being a psudo clone to just ruining someones entire deck by stealing their commanders abilities. Being Rakdos is probably the best thing going for this "clone" given he can make a perfect wubrg alongside derevi.

* [[Agatha’s Soul Cauldron]] Probably one of the strongest cards given to sisay within the past year. This card can enable alot of win lines with you not having all the colors necessary. The main enabler for this is [[Gaeas Cradle]] with five creatures and cauldron you can go up the Flash Unicorn or Oath of Nicol line with ease. Thats best case scenario, worst case it allows cards like mana vault, sol ring or ancient tomb to help you spin with Sisay. Lets say your bloom tender or faeburrow elder gets sent to grave, FRET NOT, for cauldron can now turn whatever creature you have on the field thats ready to activate to put on the Rainbow Dork hat and start your tutor chains. 

* [[Cultist of the absolute]] Previously mentioned it is great for getting Orrery when spinning for the win but I have said this for a long time. Cultist is a SISAY card. It does not matter what build you have, pod, clam, even casual. One mana to boost her power to +4 is outrageously good. Easy to spin for at low power, E tutor or anyway you may need it. This is the ONLY legendary card that can get a Naked Sisay to the her most realistic requirement to spin for any legend in the deck sans orrery. 

* [[Kutzil Malamet Exemplar]] the best kitty, on the surface it gives us another Silence effect like Teferi it also doubles as a draw enabler if you can connect with a modified creature. Sisay being the easiest but the hierarchs and cauldron buffs also allow you to draw if you can connect. 

* [[saheeli rai]] not only a win con through infinite walker activations with her +1 but also a haste enabler for our rainbow dorks! 

* [[Lavinia Azorius renegade]] a classic stax piece that can really hinder the current turbo meta, with all the etalis, rals, rogsis and more going around its nice to have a calm legend that can keep them in check even if only for a bit.

* [[Spider-Woman, Stunning Savior]] Keeping in line with a new age idea of slowing others down so we can keep up ill be including this one. 

* [[Ashiok Dream render]] An old staple back in the day that bogs grave yards and ruins tutors. It was a house once upon a time and sticking with the mindset of slowing down the table it's been a nice fit to come back to. 

===endpanel
===panel: Non Sisay Tutors

* [[Demonic Tutor]] the good ol fashioned bread and butter for any cedh deck. Anything you want it is yours for the low cost of one and a black. 

* [[Enlightened Tutor]] for just one mana you can tutor to top any artifact or enchantment you may need. that can range from a simple sol ring, to enduring vitality, and even orrery if you have the chance to win with it! Grabbing Cauldron is also one of the more important targets for this spell. Worst case you grab a Rhystic study in the early game and let the RNGods choose your fate! 

* [[Vampiric Tutor]] Another classic spell for any aspiring CEDH deck not much to say past it can grab whatever you could need at a moments notice. 

* [[Crop rotation]] Did you want to find Cradle even sooner? Here is how we get there beyond just spinning Sisay. Easy enough to warrant considering how dependent our list has become on it. 
 
* [[Chord of calling]] Truly a tutor of all time, knowing that we typically need haste on our turn makes this card huge for end step plays! not much more to say, its great! 

* [[Neoform]] a classic tutor that has just helped lately, turning your dorks into a bloom tender at the worst has made this card very valuable plus maybe you want to sac your big dork like tender or selvala to eat for agathas soul cauldron synergy!

===endpanel
===panel: Counter Spells, Protection and Removal 


* [[Flusterstorm]] a debated card before but a necessary one in my opinion. A bit linear so it can only stop instants and sorceries but figure the average cedh deck is using those cards to go for those wins! When to deploy it can be tricky given you need a high enough storm count but as you play the card you will find the right windows. 

* [[Mental Misstep]] the classic one cmc to stop a one cmc counterspell. A very important card for any cedh deck but what to counter is always where i have issue with new folks who take up cedh. While hitting a sol ring, mana vault, maybe even a mana dork but I dont agree in that case. I reserve this card to counter other missteps, other counterspells, demonic consult, or any of the one cmc tutors.

* [[Orims Chant]] some old world 60 card tech, why not have another silence effect esp one that says STFU to at least the turbo player

* [[Suspend]] a current replacement for swords, doing nearly the same thing for how fast cedh games go plus it pitches to force! 

* [[Strix serenade]] Previously mentioned Sisays biggest concerns are [[Opposition agent]] and [[Cursed totem]] and this card counters those perfectly! For those reasons alone THIS IS A SISAY CARD. Simple as that. However it obviously can counter other pesky things, weathered runestone, thoracle, commanders and maybe even a rivaling Sisays planewalkers as they cast them. 

* [[Swan Song]] Another classic counterspell for cedh. Stopping other interaction, Rhystics, smothering tithes, underworld breaches, kill spells etc. 

* [[Fierce guardianship]] When we have a commander centric deck like ours it is a no brainer that you would include a card like this. Either to stop someone elses win attempt or protect your own for the cost of nothing! 

* [[Mindbreak trap]] A card i had debated in the past until my good friend Jorman (Twang) told me one of the most important things Sisay needs to do, Get back to my turn. This card having the ability to be free is exactly what that mindset encapsulates. Sure it may not be active every turn that is its biggest downside, but when a stack gets big enough, or a player goes for a multi spell cast turn its an easy slot to keep within the deck

* [[Force of will]] Once again a cedh staple. However in Sisay this has been a debated card. For any generic five color deck slotting this  in is a no brainer however i dont agree for this deck. As a minimum requirement to play this card i suggest 30% of blue within your deck. Why this is, is because many of our enablers for win cons happen to be blue, teferi, derevi, nicol etc and if you need to pitch those to not lose the game well now you could be sitting there unable to win and just king making. Just something to consider should you choose to run this. 

* [[Legolas's Quick Reflexes]] A very flexible interaction piece. It can protect at split second, but also untap so you can continue a tutor chain or even be a removal spell if you have a tappable creature! Being nearly uncounterable this card is just so solid in our mana dork based deck. 

* [[Silence]] SHHHH for the other people at the table. Need to prevent someone from winning on their entire turn? Need to keep out the pests going for your own win? Well silence has your back for a singular white mana. 

* [[Deflecting swat]] Same as fierce, when you have a commander centric deck it is important to have free interaction on hand. Need to stop someone from targeting you or something you control? Easy peasy. Is someone about to win with a curiosity or freed from the reel? move that spell over to something less worrisome. 

* [[Into the flood maw]] new to the deck but already has proven itself. For one blue you can bounce any creature to their owners hand, like an imposing oppo! Or if you don't mind handing out a little fishy you can bounce any nonland permanent! Teach a man to fish or something. 

* [[abrupt decay]] since the dawn of this deck, decay has been a staple. removing the two most pesky spells of oppo and totem for only two uncounterable mana makes this card terribly important. 

* [[Colossal skyturtle]] another solid channel removal spell that can bounce a creature but can also be used to recur any pieces you may be unable to ge back from the grave! 



===endpanel
===endaccordion
===endpanel
===panel: Gameplay information (Under Construction)
===accordion
===panel: Opening Hands
Ideally, we play a bunch of dorks out as fast as possible.  We don't aim to combo turn two, but we do need to ramp aggressively, ideally ending with double the mana per turn (turn 1 we end with two, turn two we end with 4, turn 3 we end with 6, etc).
Example hands:
===endpanel

===panel: What Stops Sisay?
Now, all of these cards are evaluated based on them being the only piece out at a time. 

**The Uh Oh cards**:  Opposition Agent, Blood Moon/Magus/Back to basics, Cursed Totems, Orcish Bowmaster, Suppression Field, weathered runestone, drana linvala, Containment priest, grafdiggers cage, torpor orb, null rod (ish) drannith, otawara and boseiju. 

These cards above are the things that can really hinder our gameplan so it may be worth using your interaction on those vs some more general issues that may arise. 


**Cards that are concerning but dont blow us out** Rule of Law, Sphere of Resistance gaddock teeg, Lavinia renegade Trinisphere, Defense Grid

these cards, while worrisome, are things that considering the decks philosophy can actually wind up helping us. The longer the game goes on the more inevitable we become and cards like these hinder others more than ourselves. sans defense grid, we can typically tutor in response to someone attempting to win so if you can keep the grid on the field for your turn it can be beneficial. 

===endpanel
===panel: Cards to be considered 



===endpanel
===panel: Card Testing/things that have been used in the past

* [[Cryptolith rite]] is a new include from testing on my "Sans" list allowing everything to be birds of paradise is great plus it cant usually be killed, worth testing for now

* [[Ruby Daring Tracker]] another simple inclusion two pips, red being the most important, and makes mana for us! 

* [[Magda Brazen Outlaw]] Not new to cedh but new to this scene is this dwarf. With [[Gene Pollinator]] [[Springleaf drum]] and all the other tap effects already built into the deck being able to sneak out chromatic orrery seems like it will be enormous! 

* [[Chromatic Orrery]] Post dockside this card has become of my most important slots in the deck. Many ask me, "why would you play something so high costed when similar effects already exist? How are you even tutoring for this?!" simple my friends. the best way to look at this is like a seven mana dockside. I have all the mana generation in the world in this build so casting it is not hard, and with delighted halfling and silence effects built in we can deploy this with ease. Now how to spin for it? [[Cultist of the absolute]] is your best friend in getting Sisay to max power necessary to grab this. 

* [[Deathrite shaman]] i always have an issue with cards that dont produce mana on their own, needing a land in grave to get anything out of this just isnt reliable enough for me to keep him at this time. plus not getting a double mana bonus from kinnan is just heart wrenching. 

* [[Assassins trophy]] with the up tick in green rog/x decks and needing more wide range removal, this has been an excellent addition. used back in the day and figure why not using it once more! 

* [[Louisoix's Sacrifice]] this hot new spell is the most "Sisay" piece I have seen in such a minute. Stifle and a negate for the low cost of one!?...and a legend. Could be a real double edged sword to sac a legend but with cauldron synergy it may be worth to sac a Sevlala or something just to get it into the graveyard. Plus realistically it's worth playing something that says we don't lose the game for the cost of a legend or three mana. 

* [[cyclonic rift]] I am always happy to see this card, removing any non land that may be in our way without being able to be swatted to your things is great. Plus being an upside of a board wipe i think is always important to have at least one in any deck.

* [[The Soul Stone]] albeit a very strong card for sisay as a whole its hard to warrant a black mana to get a black mana out of it when we need more diverse mana as a whole, may consider more of the stones as they release and hopefully stay 2 cmc 

* [[Talon gates of madara]] if you run crop rot this is a fine card to have in your list but its been cut for the time being to keep early mulls lean and clean 

* [[touch the spirit realm]] a great channel removal spell, need to remove an artifact or creature for a turn? look no further than this hard to interact with channel effect. Dont be afraid to cast this spell either! Using it as an oblivion ring is never a bad idea if you are confident it must be done. 

* [[Galadriel's Dismissal]] New to the cedh scene, this card provides something so unique that most cards can't. Being a Removal spell for pesky creatures for even just a turn so you could win is a great use for it BUT you can always use it as a protection spell! Saving just one creature from a kill spell or better yet if someone attempts a [[toxic deluge]] which i have been seeing far too often, for its overload ability you could protect all of your creatures! Consider this card the next time you brew a white cedh list.

* [[Swords to plowshares]] If you are playing white you play this card. Removing any creature for one mana is always a worthy slot and exiling them for ever is the best part of this spell. 

* [[Skrelv Defector Mite]] Post dockside bans our mana dorks are the most important things to have survive. In order for that to occur we must protect them by any means! Skrelv is a perfect cheap option for just that, while also insulating our [[ mox opal ]] count. He also comes with a niche win con! Pending what you have access to, if you have Infinite mana and infinite flickers, you can reset skrelv and put infinite toxic 1 on any creature you have and give it protection from whatever you may need to connect with each opponent. its RARE that you should ever consider this line but its a win nonetheless. 

* [[Marvin Murderous Mimic]] Newly added thanks to Duskmourn this little guy is everything we could ask for in these post dockside times. Insulating Mox Opal is always great but he could also be considered a psudo haste enabler. Figure if you have marvin out for some time so it has the ability to activate, once you drop a fresh bloom tender or Faeburrow, Marvin is at the ready to get the ball rolling. He also enables a combo line with [[Ioreth the healing house]] which i am not currently on but works as a way to untap infinitely with mana to chain into any combo you choose. 

* [[Ertai Resurrected]] - Look.  Mystic Snake and Frilled Mystic aren't playable. I get that. However, Ertai does so much more than those cards. He’s legendary, he’s a piece of removal, he’s a stifle, he’s a counterspell.  Makes sisay a 4/4. You can lock the game out with Ertai and control the table. I was very hesitant to include him at first, but after playing him, I can say he absolutely SLAPS.   Also, there is a really funny thing you can do where you can Animate someone’s One Ring with Karn, flicker Ertai to “destroy it” and force the TOR player to draw a card. And you kill them by forcing them to deck themselves. Which kills and gets around TOR Protection. This King doubles as a counter spell and a kill spell for anything that may get in the way. His high cmc  is not always easy to spin for using sisays ability but he is necessary nonetheless! Plus as previously mentioned he also can be a great in a pinch win con with the ertai lock!

* [[Hidden Footblade]] New to the deck i think this card provides some great value considering our best win cons require haste, BT, Faeburrow and Selvala. being a red pipped card as well helps immensely with BT and fae as well! Plus it never hurts to up the mopal count! 

* [[Pippin guard of the citadel]] much like skrelv, protection is paramount. Being two pips this guy helps so much to protect the team while also coming with his own little protection on ward 1. The biggest upside of this card vs Skrelv is that it protects from "Card Type" that means beyond the typical instants and sorceries it also protects from [[Otawara soaring city]] and [[Boseiju who endures]] 

* [[Worldly tutor]] This card has surprisingly been one for debate as of late for how necessary it is in the deck. The easy answer is to get a dork. Bloom tender or faeburrow are the easiest but what if thats not what you need? Well that is just fine. Grabbing OBM, Enduring Vitality or maybe even pippin if you need a protection in the early game. It really comes down to what is going on in game for you to clearly decide what you may need. 

* [[Eladamri's Call]] This is a new addition that when we have cards like OBM and Ertai that having something at instant speed tutor to hand can be very strong. Just being the creature deck that we are having the ability to put any creature to hand in prep for the next turn will always be a fine choice. 

* [[Dispel]] a great cheap counterspell that either protects against others interaction pieces or stops pesky cards like demonic consult, tainted pact, ad naus etc
